---
title: "Applied Differential Privacy"
toc: false
title-block-style: none
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>#quarto-document-content > header#title-block-header { display: none; }</style>
---

::: {.hero}
<p class="hero-title">Applied Differential Privacy</p>

<p class="tagline">A hands-on course on differential privacy and privacy-preserving machine learning.</p>
:::

::: {.offering-banner}
<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED OFFERING BANNER -->
**Fall 2026**

- **Dates:** 2026-10-20 – 2027-01-26
- **Meeting:** Tuesdays 10:00–12:00
- **Instructor(s):** [Course staff](https://github.com/applied-dp-course)
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED OFFERING BANNER -->
:::

::: {.quick-links}

::: {.quick-link-card}
[**Schedule**](schedule.qmd)

Week-by-week lectures, slides, and assignments.
:::

::: {.quick-link-card}
[**Lectures**](lectures.qmd)

Self-study notebooks, decks, and embedded apps.
:::

::: {.quick-link-card}
[**Assignments**](assignments.qmd)

Problem sets grouped by subject.
:::

::: {.quick-link-card}
[**Tools**](tools.qmd)

Standalone and lecture interactives.
:::

:::

Lectures pair narrative slides with **live, in-browser interactivity** — every computation is
imported from the course library [`libdpy`](https://github.com/applied-dp-course/pub_lib); this
site only presents and wires it together.

## Featured interactive

::: {.featured-interactive}
Explore the Gaussian privacy tradeoff (same widget as Lecture 3). The plot loads automatically
after the page is ready; the first load downloads a Python runtime (~34 MB).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
from scipy.stats import norm

from libdpy.visualization.privacy_plots import PrivacyPlot

PrivacyPlot(
    sensitivity=1,
    std=1.5,
    distribution_types=[norm],
).embed()
```
:::

::: {.standalone-pointer}
**Not taking the course?** Browse the [Tools](tools.qmd) gallery or read standalone posts on the
[Blog](blog/index.qmd).
:::
